alerts.name: High Error Rate
alerts.description: Triggers when the percentage of failed requests exceeds 5% over a 5-minute window
alerts.query: requests | where timestamp > ago(5m) | summarize FailureRate = (countif(success == false) * 100.0) / count() | where FailureRate > 5
alerts.notes: High error rates may indicate a system-wide issue. Investigate immediately.

alerts.name: Slow API Responses
alerts.description: Triggers when average API response time exceeds 2000ms over a 5-minute window
alerts.query: requests | where timestamp > ago(5m) | summarize AvgDuration = avg(duration) | where AvgDuration > 2000
alerts.notes: Slow responses impact user experience. Check database performance and API dependencies.

alerts.name: Failed Authentication Spike
alerts.description: Triggers when authentication failures exceed 10 attempts in 1 minute
alerts.query: customEvents | where name == 'AuthenticationFailure' and timestamp > ago(1m) | summarize count() | where count_ > 10
alerts.notes: Multiple failed authentication attempts may indicate a brute force attack. Review security logs.

alerts.name: Database Connection Failures
alerts.description: Triggers when any database dependency fails
alerts.query: dependencies | where type == 'SQL' and success == false and timestamp > ago(1m) | summarize count() | where count_ > 0
alerts.notes: Database connection failures will cause application-wide issues. Check database availability and connection strings.

alerts.name: High Memory Usage
alerts.query: customMetrics | where name == 'MemoryUsage' and timestamp > ago(5m) | summarize avg(value) | where avg_value > 80
alerts.notes: High memory usage may lead to application instability. Consider scaling resources or investigating memory leaks.

alerts.name: Low Disk Space
alerts.description: Triggers when available disk space is less than 10GB
alerts.query: customMetrics | where name == 'DiskSpaceFree' and timestamp > ago(10m) | summarize min(value) | where min_value < 10
alerts.notes: Low disk space can prevent logging and cause application failures. Clean up logs or expand storage.

alerts.name: Hangfire Job Failures
alerts.description: Triggers when Hangfire background jobs fail more than 5 times in 10 minutes
alerts.query: customEvents | where name startswith 'Hangfire' and customDimensions.Status == 'Failed' and timestamp > ago(10m) | summarize count() | where count_ > 5
alerts.notes: Failed background jobs may affect scheduled tasks like email notifications and video transcoding.

alerts.name: Video Upload Failure Rate High
alerts.description: Triggers when video upload failure rate exceeds 20% over 15 minutes
alerts.query: customEvents | where name in ('VideoUploadSuccess', 'VideoUploadFailure') and timestamp > ago(15m) | summarize FailureRate = (countif(name == 'VideoUploadFailure') * 100.0) / count() | where FailureRate > 20
alerts.notes: High video upload failure rates may indicate storage issues or network problems.

alerts.name: Exception Spike
alerts.description: Triggers when exception count exceeds 20 in 5 minutes
alerts.query: exceptions | where timestamp > ago(5m) | summarize count() | where count_ > 20
alerts.notes: Sudden spike in exceptions indicates a critical issue. Check recent deployments and error logs.

alerts.name: Health Check Failures
alerts.description: Triggers when any health check reports unhealthy status
alerts.query: traces | where message contains 'health check' and message contains 'Unhealthy' and timestamp > ago(2m) | summarize count() | where count_ > 0
alerts.notes: Failed health checks indicate degraded service. Investigate immediately before user impact.

email-admins.description: Sends email notifications to system administrators

email-devops.description: Sends email notifications to DevOps team

email-security.description: Sends email notifications to security team

notes: This configuration file defines alert rules for Azure Application Insights.
1. Go to Azure Portal > Application Insights > Alerts > New alert rule
2. Select the signal (metric or log query) matching the query above
3. Set the threshold and time window as specified
Adjust thresholds based on your application's baseline performance.
Test alerts after configuration to ensure notifications work correctly.
